assertions.assertion: Response identifies ASP.NET Core Data Protection as the root cause of the decryption failure
assertions.rationale: Data Protection is commonly known to cause decryption issues during framework upgrades

assertions.assertion: Response explains that the default application name (content root path discriminator) changed between .NET versions
assertions.rationale: The specific content root path discriminator change across .NET versions is an obscure detail

assertions.assertion: Response recommends calling SetApplicationName() on AddDataProtection() with an explicit application name
assertions.rationale: SetApplicationName is a commonly recommended fix for Data Protection isolation issues

assertions.assertion: Response mentions ensuring old Data Protection keys are still accessible (not deleted or rotated away)
assertions.rationale: While logical, this specific guidance about old keys being accessible is often not explicitly mentioned without migration docs
